RULING: PLAINTIFF LINDA PONCHIONE, BRYAN PONCHIONE, LISA MENIKETTI Motion To Tax Defendant O'Reilly Auto Enterprises, LLC (Fka CSK Auto, Inc.)'s Memorandum Of Costs

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to tax defendant O'Reilly Auto Enterprises, LLC (fka CSK Auto, Inc.)'s memorandum of costs is granted in part and denied in part, as follows: The motion is granted as to "Designated Defense Counsel" costs totaling $6,224.90, which are taxed because defendant failed to demonstrate how they are allowable pursuant to C.C.P. 1033.5(a)(10) or (a)(16). The motion is granted as to "Medical Records" costs totaling $841.49, which are taxed because defendant failed to demonstrate how they are allowable pursuant to C.C.P. 1033.5(a)(10) or (a)(16). The motion is granted in part and denied in part as to "Court Transcript - MSJ." $354 is taxed per defendant's concession and the motion is denied as to the remaining $187.50, which is an allowable court reporter fee cost. (C.C.P. 1033.5(a)(11).) The motion is granted as to the "MSJ - Exhibits" cost totaling $185.15, which is taxed because defendant failed to demonstrate how this cost is allowable pursuant to C.C.P. 1033.5 and, even if such cost, or a portion of such cost, was allowable, defendant failed to demonstrate that the cost was reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation and was reasonable in amount. (C.C.P. 1033.5(c).) The total amount taxed is $7,605.54. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552. (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

RULING:
DEFENDANT CURTIS WIGGINS ENTERPRISES, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Curtis Wiggins Enterprises, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings of plaintiffs' third and fourth causes of action is denied. Defendant's motion is denied as untimely pursuant to plaintiffs' objection. (C.C.P. 438(e).) Even if it is timely, the motion is denied because defendant failed to demonstrate that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it. (C.C.P. 438(c)(1)(B)(ii).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit 1 and granted in part as to Exhibit 2. The Court will take judicial notice of the existence of the April 7, 2017 order granting CSK Auto, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication but will not take judicial notice of the truth of facts and findings stated in that order. Finally, even if the Court takes judicial notice of the entirety of Exhibit 2, that ruling does not constitute law of the case. Order signed in open court. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, Tel. 415.898.1555, for plaintiff and opposing party; No other appearance.

RULING: DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The court's tentative ruling was uncontested and adopted as follows: Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Even assuming that defendant was not the proximate cause of decedent Cary Ponchione's death and that plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue whether defendant's negligent wrong caused a mental condition which resulted in an uncontrollable impulse to commit suicide, that determination does not demonstrate that the action has no merit. (C.C.P. 437c(a), (c).) The cause of decedent's death - the sole issue raised on summary judgment - is only relevant for the wrongful death causes of action and is not fatal to the two survival causes of action. Defendant moved solely for summary judgment and did not alternatively seek summary adjudication of separate causes of action. (C.C.P. 1010; CRC 3.1110(a), 3.1350(b), (d).) Therefore the Court cannot grant summary judgment. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot as plaintiffs have dismissed that claim against defendant. The Court will not consider plaintiffs' late-filed supplemental opposition. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: G. Gonzales, Not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

RULING:
DEFENDANT STM AUTOMOTIVE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant STM Automotive, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint pursuant to C.C.P. 435 and 436 is denied in its entirety. As to the motion to strike the third (negligence I - wrongful death) and fourth (products liability - wrongful death) causes of action, a motion to strike an entire cause of action is improper. (See Quiroz v. Seventh Avenue Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281; Ferraro v. Camarlinghi (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 509, 528.) Defendant's motion to strike the sixth cause of action (negligence II - wrongful death) is denied as moot as that cause of action is not alleged against defendant. As to defendant's motion to strike items nos. 1, 3, 4 and 5 from plaintiffs' request for damages, pursuant to this Court's ruling on defendant's demurrer, valid causes of action are stated in the First Amended Complaint and defendant failed to demonstrate that the challenged damages are not supported by the causes of action pleaded. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Linda Ponchione, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Cary Ponchione, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT STM AUTOMOTIVE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant STM Automotive, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint pursuant to C.C.P. 430.10(e) and 340.2 is overruled in its entirety. Defendant's special demurrer to plaintiffs' third (negligence I - wrongful death) and fourth (products liability - wrongful death) causes of action is overruled. Although plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint was filed beyond the asbestos wrongful death limitations period enunciated in C.C.P. 340.2(c), the third and fourth causes of action are not time barred because they relate back to the timely filing of plaintiffs' initial complaint. Further, the First Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to demonstrate that decedent's death was caused by his exposure to asbestos-containing products and materials and subsequent mesothelioma and that his suicide was not an independent intervening cause, an argument that defendant did not explicitly make in its demurrer. Defendant's demurrer to the sixth cause of action (negligence II - wrongful death) is overruled as moot as that cause of action is not alleged against defendant. (See defendant's Exhibit B at p. 29:3-10 and Exhibit C at p. 1:23-24.) Defendant has 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)

George H. Irwin of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC, as successor-by-merger to Borg-Warner Corporation
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Linda Ponchione as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Cary Ponchione, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC LLC, AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC LLC's demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint ("FAC") for wrongful death and survival is overruled. Pursuant to plaintiffs' opposition, the Court disregards the demurrer due to defendant's violations of C.C.P. section 430.60 and CRC Rules 3.1112 and 3.1320. However even assuming that the Court could consider the demurrer, it would be overruled. Based on defendant's Notice of Demurrer and Memorandum of Points and Authorities, defendant appears to attempt to bring only a general demurrer to the entire FAC, which contains both survival and wrongful death causes of action. The complaint allegations demonstrate that at a minimum plaintiffs' survival causes of action are not barred by the applicable statute of limitations, and thus a general demurrer to the entire FAC would be overruled. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
